symptoms.symptom_name: Tremor and cold sweat (adrenergic) shortly after the medication round

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tremor and diaphoresis are the classic counter-regulatory adrenergic response to a falling glucose.
Catecholamine surge from hypoglycaemia drives tachycardia and a pounding heartbeat.
Palpitations are non-specific; require a cardiac screen but are explained here by hypoglycaemia.
Neuroglycopenia plus relative hypotension produce presyncope.
A duplicated antihypertensive dose contributes to the near-syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reported insulin administration error (rapid-acting given instead of long-acting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An excess rapid-acting insulin dose is the direct mechanistic cause of the hypoglycaemia.
A second same-day antihypertensive dose contributes to the relative hypotension.

loinc_ref.display: Glucose [Mass/volume] in Capillary blood by Glucometer

investigation_details.expected_result: 3.1 mmol/L at presentation, 5.8 mmol/L 15 minutes after oral carbohydrate
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypoglycaemia unrecognised and untreated; the AI cannot obtain it remotely and must direct the on-site nurse to measure it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A measured capillary glucose of 3.1 mmol/L with matching symptoms confirms hypoglycaemia (Whipple's triad).

symptoms.symptom_name: Relative hypotension BP 95/55 with tachycardia HR 110

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective relative hypotension — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from near-syncope, not a measured BP.
Absence of chest pain lowers the probability of ACS, though not excluded in a diabetic; explained by hypoglycaemia.
No focal deficit and normal speech argue strongly against acute stroke as the cause of the dizziness.
Afebrile with no source and a clear iatrogenic trigger makes sepsis unlikely.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Adrenergic hypoglycaemia cluster after an insulin error
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tremor, sweating, palpitations and presyncope minutes after an excess rapid-acting insulin dose, with a confirmatory low capillary glucose, is the defining constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms hypoglycaemia and guides treatment; the single most important immediate measure, obtainable by the on-site nurse.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat capillary glucose after treatment and 1-2 hourly monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapid-acting insulin outlasts a single carbohydrate dose, so recurrence must be detected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person medical review of the medication chart and vitals
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To reconcile the error, measure blood pressure, and formally suspend implicated doses.
Not required unless chest pain or cardiac features develop — the picture is explained by hypoglycaemia; over-investigation is unnecessary but a re-escalation edge exists.
